comment.body: Hard to believe this chat is consumed with expiration terminology.  When a market forecaster is non-specific/unclear about the expiration to which they refer, it's so they can declare victory no matter what the outcome.  The real quarterly expiration is based on futures (third Friday of March, June, September, and December).  Month end is month end.
comment.raw_body: Hard to believe this chat is consumed with expiration terminology. comment.body:
Last feb it didn’t have the march Opex. So that’s why 21 vs 31 

Basically she’s saying that we’re in a 2022 scenario. In 2022 we rallied to quarterly Opex which is the 31st / April 4th not the monthly Opex. Last time in April 2022 it gapped up 10% before dumping to 200 WMA. So yes it matters.

comment.body:
Fed was owned :)) ...again
I show you something important.
Here I coded tech 20d rolling alpha and you can see the alpha's ROC with its weekly avg. 
The 1 week change is -15.41% despite the SPX slow positive drift. But look, despite the very last upward move in the alpha curve looks small, its weight is bigger than it seems. We should see NVDA and AMD alpha keeping a positive trend into OpEx. (NVDA doesn't look too good) Otherwise it is actually a temporal positive correction.
The question is if this correction's gonna hold into Friday or into month end. (My estimated odds lean towards suppressive post-OpEx, but we need to keep our eyes open).

I will cover this in the coming post, but don't forget that stagflation is still there and the dovish tone doesn't make good for the inflationary pressures! Trump doesn't want another cycle. 
The net downward move is not done yet. Wider bias remains. I use these positive corrections to scale into puts as I kept saying it before.
